families.economic_thesis: ETF relative strength, skip-window momentum, and short-horizon reversal can capture persistent fund rotation without stock-level selection.
families.next_action: Mine full-history CN_ETF relative momentum, skip-momentum, and reversal factors with walk-forward gates.

families.economic_thesis: ETF flows leave tradable traces in volume, amount, turnover stability, and liquidity crowding; costs and participation must be modeled from the start.
families.next_action: Mine liquidity, turnover stability, and capacity-aware low-turnover factors on the full CN_ETF universe.

families.economic_thesis: ETF rotation should adapt to volatility compression, drawdown pressure, trend fragility, and defensive-asset regimes.
families.next_action: Mine low-volatility, downside-risk, drawdown-control, and regime-gated ETF factors.

families.economic_thesis: Stock-level moneyflow can still help when aggregated into ETF theme breadth, industry diffusion, and risk-appetite features aligned to ETF holdings or themes.
families.next_action: Use CN moneyflow only after aggregation into ETF-level breadth or theme-flow features.

families.economic_thesis: Fund share changes, scale proxies, NAV behavior, and premium/discount proxies may identify ETF demand pressure and crowded exits.
families.next_action: Probe daily etf_share_size share-change, scale-change, NAV, and premium/discount proxies where Tushare permissions and coverage are sufficient.

families.economic_thesis: Stock moneyflow had a plausible behavioral thesis, but direct stock-selection evidence repeatedly failed capacity, cost, OOS, and tail-IC gates.
families.next_action: Keep as auxiliary ETF-level aggregate input only; do not allocate another primary factor-mining batch to direct CN stock moneyflow.
